summary:
The patient’s presentation suggests potential chronic progressive external ophthalmoplegia over generalized myasthenia gravis based primarily on absent anti-Acetylcholine Receptor antibody presence; however, further investigation including detailed neuromuscular function tests would be necessary to confirm this diagnosis definitively. \boxed{C}

This summary captures the essential aspects by focusing on ruling out one condition favoring another through logical deduction using available evidence.
